Code fragment: dsl.select().from(ARTICLE).where(ARTICLE.ID.eq(id))
Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the ARTICLE table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., `select(ARTICLE.ID, ARTICLE.TITLE, ...)`.

Code fragment: ARTICLE.TITLE.like("%" + request.getSearch() + "%")
Explanation: Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides (`%search%`) for text search is inefficient as it cannot use indexes and performs poorly on large datasets. Consider using a full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's `tsvector`/`tsquery`, Elasticsearch, or a dedicated search engine.
